So essentially that's the basic framework for your scripts. And once you finalize your script, there's only one thing left to do and that's training drill. That's it. You've got to read those scripts out loud and keep right on practicing again and again and again until you get to that level of unconscious competence where you can literally do something without having to focus any of your own mental energy on it. It happens automatically. And remember, I don't expect you to get this perfectly the first time through. It takes some practice, but there's also that good enough factor I've spoken about, meaning even if you're only okay at writing a script, it will still dramatically increase your closing rate. And lastly, before I go here, I just want you to remember that I've spent almost 30 years writing scripts and I still torture myself over every single word, every single paragraph, and every single transition when I write a script. Remember being you should model my scripts and apply them to your own industry, okay, but read them back yourself and make sure that they make perfect sense in all the transitions and all the pros are absolutely seamless. Now, of course, it takes a bit of time to master, but I promise you that it's definitely a skill that's worth it. So spend a good amount of time with this and use the structure and the language patterns I've given you here and then just drill, drill, drill. Okay, and I'll see you in the next session. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye.
